What's going on everyone? Welcome to the pre-launch checklist. This is what you should get done before you start orienting and beginning the fulfillment process. So if you have sold and you're like going into the last couple of days, you need to go through this ASAP and get this done. So this is your checklist. There's a couple of things that we've added to it, but you can download this from the portal. Make sure that you print out 200 copies of your contract slash waiver. That way we can make sure that you never have an issue of not having a contract is the biggest pain in the ass. I used to have that. We'd print one out every time it was pain. So just make sure you print those out ahead of time. And if you don't want to do 200, then just do 130% of your desired number of signups. So if you're like, I want to have 50 signups, then print out 65 contracts and then that should because you'll have something you start and stop or you toss or whatever. Next, print out copies of the mail plans, one of each at the very least. And if you are, so this is more for if you're doing the six week challenge printing out and there's only one page, the personalized page is what you would be wanting to print out. If you are doing the 42 day, you can print out the entire plan if you'd like to. But at the very least, you want to have a full booklet of each one of them just as to hold and just mix everything look more impressive. So after you've printed out the meal plans, get a cell phone with a local area code, which can be a burner, you can get those at T mobile for 100 bucks. Or you can use the Twilio setup that we have through front app to text that way. Either ways up to you. I liked having a burner phone personally, but it is literally 100% up to you. Either way, you need to be able to call a text. Make sure that you get nine clipboards, eight hooks, mountable at the gym in line. And so one of those clipboards is going to be in the lobby, which is the sign sheet. The other clipboard will be on the salesman's desk, but he will hang that up at the end of the day, which will be the way in time. And then you'll have week zero through five. And then you'll have the way out clip there as well. Six set of the sales area in the gym. So table tablecloth, six foldable chairs. If you don't already have a selling area, and you need to also make sure that you print out the foreign afterbooks. And that you set up the sales room the way that I suggested in the selling portion of this training will increase the amount of money you make and make your sales a lot easier. Seven staff education, you want to make sure that they have watched especially the nutrition FAQs and nutritional orientations, which is just kind of the breakdown of how the nutrition plan works. You want to make sure that they have watched that so that everyone is on the same page. So they're not answering different questions, different ways. Or you would have one point of contact that they route all the questions to. But I would still highly recommend you watch it with the entire staff so they know what's going on. And they should also watch the accountability presentations. They know what the roles are going to be and what these people are going to be getting from an accountability standpoint. Eight from a service delivery standpoint in terms of sessions and session difficulty, you want to make sure that the desired amount of members that you can basically accommodate the amount of people that you're going to sign up. So increase the number of sessions if you need to, if you have a smaller facility, if you do not, then make sure that you have enough room for it and that you adjust your programming so that beginners can start out. So if you are a large group training, like a bootcamp type facility, this won't be as much of an issue because most of those workouts are done with more regressive style trainings, you're not doing complex barbell lifts. If you do have something like an affiliate box or a strength and conditioning studio where a lot of barbell lifting is done in group settings, then I would recommend having two tiers of programming, RX or prescribed or lead whatever you want to call it, the high level and then a regressive version or remedial version below. An example that would be like if you were doing a back squat with a barbell for your primary lift, then maybe the beginners would do a goblin squat with a kettlebell. Or if they're doing barbell bench press, you might want to have them do push-ups. So you want to use the same muscle groups but just do remedial versions of them and then that allows the members or the prospect, the challengers, detox program people, etc., people who are on the front end of your program, to have multiple ways to progress. So even if they're not losing weight, they can still get stronger and that's something that you can cite at the sale to show progress beyond just weight loss, etc. In addition to that, make sure that you print out one of each of the sign-in sheets, one of each of the weigh-in sheets, one or five of the client check-in sheets, which is the weekly weigh-in sheets and then one weigh-out sheets. You want to make sure that each of those clipboards has the sheet printed out on it. And then like I said, I think you should print out one of each plant in its entirety in color minimum just because it just looks awesome. Beyond that, make sure that you're following the sales room instructions. I'm saying this again because people still do it because it takes work. But I'm telling you, every single person, like when people come into legacy, they start doing it and then they're like, I have no idea why I didn't do that. So be one of the smart ones. Take the couple hours, go to Kinko's, print out before and afters, put them in a book, and use them in your lobby as a weighting rip thing, use them as a selling tool, and then put them all over your walls for you to have overwhelming social proof. And that concludes our Pulitzer Checklist.
